description: Transform BU-CLI into an insane-tier AI coding assistant with rich terminal output, intelligent context management, git superpowers, and elite developer tools

userStories.title: Add glamour dependency for rich markdown rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the charmbracelet/glamour library added so we can render beautiful markdown in the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Run 'go get github.com/charmbracelet/glamour' from the project root
go.mod includes github.com/charmbracelet/glamour as a dependency
Run 'go mod tidy' to clean up transitive deps
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added glamour v0.10.0 with chroma, lipgloss, termenv, goldmark transitive deps. go mod tidy clean. Build passes.

userStories.title: Create markdown rendering module in ui package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a RenderMarkdown function that takes raw markdown text and returns ANSI-formatted terminal output using glamour.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/ui/markdown.go with a RenderMarkdown(content string) string function
Uses glamour with DarkStyle by default and auto-detects terminal width
Falls back to returning raw content if glamour rendering fails
Adds a RenderMarkdownWithTheme(content string, dark bool) string variant that respects the current ui theme (dark vs light)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created internal/ui/markdown.go with RenderMarkdown and RenderMarkdownWithTheme. Uses glamour.WithStandardStyle (dark/light) and auto-detects terminal width. Falls back to raw content on error.

userStories.title: Add /render command to toggle rich markdown output mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to toggle between raw streaming output and buffered rich-rendered markdown output so I can choose the experience I prefer.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'RenderMode bool' field to the Config struct in cmd/root.go
Add a /render command that toggles cfg.RenderMode and prints confirmation

When RenderMode is true: in agent.go Run(), buffer all streamed tokens into a strings.Builder instead of printing them raw, show a spinner during generation, then after streaming completes call ui.RenderMarkdown on the buffered content and print the result
When RenderMode is false: keep current behavior (raw token streaming)
Add /render to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Persist RenderMode in config.json via the 'render_mode' JSON field
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added RenderMode to Config, /render toggle command, buffered markdown rendering in agent Run() using ui.RenderMarkdown. Autocompleter and help updated.

userStories.title: Add token estimation module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility to estimate token counts for messages so we can manage context windows intelligently instead of counting messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal/tokens/counter.go in a new 'tokens' package

Function EstimateTokens(text string) int — estimates tokens using the heuristic: split on whitespace and punctuation, count ~1 token per 4 characters for English text (len(text)/4 as a floor approximation)
Function EstimateMessages(msgs []ollama.Message) int — sums token estimates across all messages (role name + content + tool call arguments if present)
Function FormatTokenCount(n int) string — returns human-readable format like '12.5K' or '1.2M'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created internal/tokens/counter.go with EstimateTokens (len/4 heuristic), EstimateMessages (sums all message content + tool call args), FormatTokenCount (human-readable K/M formatting).

userStories.title: Replace message-count compaction with token-aware compaction
userStories.description: As a user, I need the auto-compaction to be based on actual estimated token usage rather than a crude 100-message limit, so context is managed intelligently across different model context windows.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go, add a 'contextLimit int' field to Agent (default 128000 tokens)
Replace the 'if len(a.messages) > 100' check in Run() with: estimate total tokens using tokens.EstimateMessages, compact when exceeding 80% of contextLimit
When compacting, remove oldest non-system messages until under 60% of contextLimit, preserving the system prompt and last 10 messages minimum
The auto-compact message now shows estimated token count: '[!] Auto-pruned to ~X tokens (limit: Y)'
Add /context-limit command to set contextLimit (e.g., '/context-limit 32000' for smaller models) and add it to the readline autocompleter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added contextLimit field (default 128K), CompactToTokens method, token-aware auto-compaction at 80% threshold compacting to 60%. Added /context-limit command. Human-readable token counts in compact message.

userStories.title: Add session metrics tracking to Agent struct
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Agent to track token usage and API call metrics throughout the session for display and analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fields to Agent struct: InputTokens int, OutputTokens int, APICallCount int, ToolCallCount int, SessionStart time.Time
Set SessionStart in the New() constructor

After each ChatStream call in Run(), increment APICallCount by 1, estimate and add input tokens (from the messages sent) to InputTokens, estimate and add output tokens (from result.Content + tool call args) to OutputTokens
After each tool execution, increment ToolCallCount by 1
Add getter methods: Stats() returning a struct with all metrics plus computed fields (total tokens, session duration, average tok/s)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added sessionStart, inputTokens, outputTokens, apiCallCount, toolCallCount fields. Metrics updated after each ChatStream and executeTool call. SessionStats struct and Stats() getter method added.

userStories.title: Add /stats command showing session metrics dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a rich dashboard of my session metrics including token usage, API calls, duration, and context window usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /stats command to handleCommand in root.go

Display formatted output showing: Model name, Session duration, API calls count, Tool calls count, Input tokens (estimated), Output tokens (estimated), Total tokens, Current context window usage (tokens used / limit), a visual progress bar for context usage using block characters
Use colored output: Green for low usage (<50%), Yellow for medium (50-80%), Red for high (>80%) context usage
Add /stats to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /stats command with full metrics dashboard: model, duration, API/tool call counts, token usage breakdown, context usage with colored progress bar (green/yellow/red).

userStories.title: Add /diff command to inject git changes into context
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly inject my current git diff into the conversation so the agent can analyze my uncommitted changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /diff command to handleCommand in root.go
Runs 'git diff' and 'git diff --cached' via exec.Command, combines output
If no changes found, prints 'No uncommitted changes detected.'

If changes found, sends the diff as a user message through ag.Run() with the prompt: 'Here are my current uncommitted git changes. Please analyze them:
```diff
{diff_output}
```'
Add /diff to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /diff command. Runs git diff + git diff --cached, combines output, injects into conversation via ag.Run() with diff prompt. Autocompleter and help updated.

userStories.title: Add /export command for conversation export to markdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export my current conversation as a readable markdown file for documentation or sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /export command to handleCommand in root.go, accepts optional filename argument
Creates ~/.bu-cli/exports/ directory if it does not exist
Default filename: 'conversation-{timestamp}.md'
Prints confirmation with file path on success
Add /export to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /export command. Writes conversation as markdown to ~/.bu-cli/exports/ with model, date, and all user/assistant/tool messages formatted. Supports custom filename argument.

userStories.title: Add /paste command for clipboard integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste clipboard contents directly into the conversation without manually copying text into the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /paste command to handleCommand in root.go
On macOS (runtime.GOOS == 'darwin'): run 'pbpaste' to get clipboard contents
On Linux: try 'xclip -selection clipboard -o', fall back to 'xsel --clipboard --output'
If clipboard is empty, print 'Clipboard is empty.'
If content found, print a preview (first 3 lines + '... ({N} total lines)'), then pass the full content as user input through ag.Run()
Add /paste to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /paste command. Uses pbpaste on macOS, xclip/xsel on Linux. Shows 3-line preview then runs content through agent. Autocompleter and help updated.

userStories.title: Add run_tests tool for automatic test execution
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to have a dedicated tool for running project tests that auto-detects the test framework, so it can verify changes without needing to know the exact command.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'run_tests' tool definition to tools.Definitions() in tools.go with parameters: 'path' (optional directory), 'filter' (optional test name filter)
Add run_tests to the Execute switch in tools.go, calling a new execRunTests function

execRunTests auto-detects the test command by checking for: go.mod -> 'go test ./...', package.json -> 'npm test', Cargo.toml -> 'cargo test', pyproject.toml/requirements.txt -> 'pytest', Gemfile -> 'bundle exec rspec'
If filter is provided, append it to the command (e.g., 'go test ./... -run {filter}')
Run the command with a 5 minute timeout, return stdout+stderr
Add run_tests to the toolSpinLabel map in agent.go with label 'Running tests...'
Add run_tests to AutoAllowed as false (requires permission)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added run_tests tool with auto-detection for Go, Rust, Node, Python, Ruby. Supports filter param. 5-min timeout. Added to definitions, Execute switch, AutoAllowed (false), and toolSpinLabel.

userStories.title: Persist tool permission preferences in config
userStories.description: As a user, I want my 'Always allow' tool permission choices to persist across sessions so I don't have to re-approve the same tools every time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'AutoAllow []string' field to Config struct in root.go with json tag 'auto_allow,omitempty'
On startup in runREPL(), after creating the agent, merge cfg.AutoAllow into the agent's autoAllow map
Add a new method Agent.AutoAllowedTools() []string that returns the names of all currently auto-allowed non-default tools
When a user selects 'Always allow' for a tool, also update the Config and auto-save to disk (call saveConfig)
Pass a callback or reference to the Agent so it can trigger config saves — add an Agent.SetOnAutoAllow(fn func(toolName string)) callback
Add /permissions command that lists all tools and whether they are auto-allowed, with the ability to toggle them
Add /permissions to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added AutoAllow []string to Config. Persisted on 'Always allow' selection via onAutoAllow callback. Added /permissions command showing auto/always/ask status for all tools. Restored on startup.

userStories.title: Improve connection error handling with friendly messages
userStories.description: As a user, I need clear, actionable error messages when things go wrong with the AI backend connection instead of raw Go error strings.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/ollama/client.go, wrap connection errors in ChatStream with user-friendly messages: if 'connection refused' -> 'Cannot connect to Ollama at {host}. Is Ollama running? Start it with: ollama serve'
If HTTP 404 -> 'Model {model} not found. Run: ollama pull {model}'
If HTTP 401/403 -> 'Authentication failed. Check your API key with /key'
If timeout -> 'Request timed out. The model may be loading. Try again in a moment.'
Add a new exported function ClassifyError(err error, host string, model string) string that returns a friendly error string
In agent.go Run(), use ClassifyError when ChatStream returns an error, before returning it to the REPL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added ClassifyError function in ollama/client.go covering connection refused, 404, 401/403, timeout, rate limit, 5xx. Added Host() getter. Agent.Run() now uses ClassifyError for user-friendly error display.

userStories.title: Add interactive model selector to /model command
userStories.description: As a user, I want /model with no arguments to show an interactive selector of available models instead of just printing the current model name.
acceptanceCriteria: When /model is called with no arguments, attempt to fetch local models from Ollama using client.ListModels()
Combine local models with the KnownCloudModels list, deduplicating by name
Display using ui.SelectOption with each model as an option (show model name and size for local models, 'cloud' tag for cloud models)
Limit to max 15 options to avoid screen overflow
If the user selects a model, update cfg.Model and ag.SetModel()
If ListModels() fails (Ollama not running), fall back to showing KnownCloudModels only with a warning
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Upgraded /model to show interactive SelectOption list when called with no args. Fetches local models from Ollama, merges with KnownCloudModels, shows size. Falls back to cloud-only on connection failure. Capped at 15 options.

userStories.title: Add /log command to inject recent git history into context
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly inject recent git commit history into the conversation so the agent understands what has changed recently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /log command to handleCommand in root.go, accepts optional count argument (default: 20)
Runs 'git log --oneline --no-decorate -N' where N is the count
If not in a git repo, prints 'Not in a git repository.'

If log output found, sends it as user message through ag.Run() with prompt: 'Here is the recent git history for this project:
```
{log_output}
```
Please review and let me know if you have any observations.'
Add /log to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /log command. Runs git log --oneline -N, injects into conversation. Supports custom count arg. Autocompleter and help updated.

userStories.title: Add /context command showing context window usage breakdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see exactly how my context window is being used, broken down by message type, so I can manage long conversations effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /context command to handleCommand in root.go
Calculate token estimates for each message category: system prompt, user messages, assistant messages, tool results
Display a formatted breakdown table showing: category name, message count, estimated tokens, percentage of total
Show a visual bar chart using block characters for each category with the ui theme colors (Purple for system, Cyan for user, Green for assistant, Yellow for tools)
Show total context usage vs the configured context limit with a progress bar
Add /context to the readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added /context command. Shows breakdown by system/user/assistant/tool with message counts and token estimates. Colored progress bar for context usage. Autocompleter updated.
